Paragraph.Text: prescribing records or information to be included in the Registry by the Agency or a responsible authority, and respecting the charging of fees for providing copies of documents contained either in a list of relevant documents contained in the Registry or in the Registry itself;

Subparagraph.Label: (h.1)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 38(1) or (2) or 53(1), prescribing the manner of designing a follow-up program;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 59(j) to (l) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i.1)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing, in the case of projects that are to be carried out outside Canada and any federal lands and that are subject to assessment of environmental effects conducted in accordance with section 8, in prescribed circumstances or on any prescribed terms and conditions,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: federal authorities that, notwithstanding subsection 5(1), are not required to conduct environmental assessments of those projects, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: federal authorities for whom the requirements under this Act in respect of those projects, other than those set out in subsections 20(1) and 37(1), are deemed to be satisfied by an assessment of the environmental effects of those projects conducted in accordance with section 8;

Paragraph.Label: (i.2)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subparagraph (i.1)(ii), varying subsection 20(1) or 37(1) in its application to federal authorities prescribed under that subparagraph;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of section 8, designating corporations referred to in section 8 individually or by class and respecting the manner in which those corporations or classes of corporations conduct assessments of the environmental effects of, and follow-up programs for, projects, as well as any action to be taken in respect of projects during the assessment process â which manners and actions may vary by corporation or class of corporation â and, for those purposes, respecting the application of the laws from time to time in force in any province;

Paragraph.Label: (j.1)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of section 8 and for projects to be carried out outside Canada and any federal lands, prescribing, in relation to any corporation designated in a regulation made under paragraph (j), any physical activity or class of physical activities in replacement of those prescribed under paragraph (b);

Paragraph.Label: (j.2)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the provisions of any Act of Parliament or any regulation made pursuant to an Act of Parliament that confer powers, duties or functions on a corporation referred to in section 8, the exercise or performance of which requires an assessment of the environmental effects under paragraph 8(1.1)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (j.3)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the circumstances in which a corporation referred to in section 8 must ensure that an assessment of the environmental effects of a project to be carried out in whole or in part on federal lands is conducted, and specifying the right or interest that it must have in the federal lands, for the purposes of paragraph 8(1.1)(e);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of section 9, respecting the manner of conducting assessments of the environmental effects of, and follow up programs for, projects, as well as any action to be taken in respect of projects during the assessment process and, for those purposes, respecting the application of the laws from time to time in force in any province;

Paragraph.Label: (k.1)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the provisions of any Act of Parliament or any regulation made pursuant to an Act of Parliament that confer powers, duties or functions on a body referred to in subsection 9(1), the exercise or performance of which requires an assessment of environmental effects under paragraph 9(2)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (k.2)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the circumstances in which an assessment of the environmental effects of a project to be carried out in whole or in part on federal lands must be conducted under paragraph 9(2)(e);

Paragraph.Label: (k.3)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of section 9.1, prescribing by class authorities other than federal authorities and respecting the manner in which those classes of authorities shall conduct assessments of the environmental effects of, and follow-up programs for, projects, as well as any action to be taken in respect of projects during the assessment process â which manners and actions may vary by class of authority â and, for those purposes, respecting the application of the laws from time to time in force in any province;

Paragraph.Label: (k.4)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the provisions of any Act of Parliament or any regulation made pursuant to an Act of Parliament that confer powers, duties or functions on an authority prescribed in regulations made under paragraph (k.3), the exercise or performance of which requires an assessment of the environmental effects under paragraph 9.1(2)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (k.5)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of paragraph 9.1(2)(e), prescribing the circumstances in which an assessment of the environmental effects of a project to be carried out in whole or in part on federal lands must be conducted, and specifying the right or interest that the authority prescribed in regulations made under paragraph (k.3) must have in the federal lands;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of section 10, designating bands individually or by class and respecting the manner in which those bands or classes of bands shall conduct assessments of the environmental effects of, and follow-up programs for, projects that are to be carried out in whole or in part on a reserve that is set apart for the use and benefit of a designated band and that is subject to the Indian Act, as well as any action to be taken in respect of projects during the assessment process, which manners and actions may vary by band or class of band;

Paragraph.Label: (l.01)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of section 10.1,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: varying the definition âprojectâ in subsection 2(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting the manner of conducting assessments of the environmental effects of, and follow-up programs for, projects for which the Canadian International Development Agency exercises a power or performs a duty or function referred to in subsection 10.1(2) and respecting any action to be taken in respect of those projects during the assessment process,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: providing that, in the case of a project in respect of which an agreement or arrangement entered into by the Canadian International Development Agency in accordance with subsection 54(2) applies, no assessment of environmental effects need be carried out by that agency,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: varying or excluding any of the provisions of section 54 in their application to the Canadian International Development Agency, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: providing for the application of section 55.4 to the Canadian International Development Agency as if it were a responsible authority;

Paragraph.Label: (l.02)
Paragraph.Text: varying or excluding any of the provisions of sections 55 to 55.3 in their application to the Canadian International Development Agency;

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 30: (1) to (4)
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Paragraphs 59(a.1), (c.1), (h.1), (i.1), (i.2), (j.1) to (j.3), (k.1) to (k.5), (l.01) and (l.02) are new. The relevant portion of section 59 reads as follows:

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing any project or class of projects for which an environmental assessment is not required where the Governor in Council is satisfied that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an environmental assessment of the project would be inappropriate for reasons of national security, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a project in relation to a physical work, the environmental effects of the project are insignificant or the contribution of the responsible authority to the project in exercising powers or performing duties or functions referred to in section 5 in relation to the project is minimal;

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the dissemination by responsible authorities of information relating to projects and the environmental assessment of projects and the establishment, maintenance and operation of a public registry, including facilities to enable the public to examine physical or electronic records contained in the registry, the time and manner in which those records may be examined or copied by the public and the charging of fees therefor, and the transfer and retention of those records after the completion of any follow-up program;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner of conducting assessments of the environmental effects of, and follow-up programs for projects for which a Crown corporation within the meaning of the Financial Administration Act or any corporation controlled by such a corporation exercises a power or performs a duty or function referred to in paragraph 5(1)(a), (b) or (c), respecting any action to be taken in respect of those projects during the environmental assessment process and, for those purposes, respecting the application of the laws from time to time in force in any province;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner of conducting assessments of the environmental effects of, and follow up programs for, projects for which The Hamilton Harbour Commissioners constituted pursuant to The Hamilton Harbour Commissioners' Act, The Toronto Harbour Commissioners constituted pursuant to The Toronto Harbour Commissioners' Act, 1911, any harbour commission established pursuant to the Harbour Commissions Act, a not for profit corporation that enters into an agreement under subsection 80(5) of the Canada Marine Act or a port authority established under that Act, exercises a power or performs a duty or function referred to in paragraph 5(1)(a), (b) or (c), respecting any action to be taken in respect of those projects during the environmental assessment process and, for those purposes, respecting the application of the laws from time to time in force in any province;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner of conducting any assessment of the environmental effects of, and follow-up programs for a project for which a person or body receives financial assistance provided by a federal authority for the purpose of enabling the project to be carried out in whole or in part on a reserve that is set apart for the use and benefit of a band and that is subject to the Indian Act, and respecting any action to be taken in respect of that project during the environmental assessment process;

Section.Text: Section 62 of the Act is amended by striking out the word âandâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: to promote and monitor compliance with this Act and the quality of assessments conducted under this Act; and

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 31:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: New. The relevant portion of section 62 reads as follows:

Section.Text: The objects of the Agency are

Subsection.Text: Subsection 63(1) of the Act is amended by striking out the word âandâ at the end of paragraph (b), by adding the word âandâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: establish and lead a quality assurance program for assessments conducted under this Act.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 32: (1)
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: New. The relevant portion of subsection 63(1) reads as follows:

Subsection.Text: In carrying out its objects, the Agency shall
Subsection 63(2) of the Act is amended by striking out the word âandâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: assist parties in building consensus and resolving disputes; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: request federal authorities, and persons and bodies referred to in sections 8 to 10, to provide information respecting assessments that they conduct under this Act.

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: New. The relevant portion of subsection 63(2) reads as follows:

Subsection.Text: In carrying out its objects, the Agency may

Section.MarginalNote: Non-application of amended provisions to assessments already commenced
Section.Text: Any environmental assessment or assessment of the environmental effects of a project commenced under the Canadian Environmental Assessment Act before this section comes into force shall be continued and completed as if this Act had not been enacted.
The provisions of this Act come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
